title: The Ones

jokes.text: Are you sure?
jokes.punchline: Employee's doubt about the customer's readiness to propose
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective doubt about customer's commitment, delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: In my experience, 'let's think about it' usually ends up with me watching Solid Gold in my basement on prom night.
jokes.setup: Discussion about what 'let's think about it' means
jokes.punchline: Specific, pathetic image of Jack's past rejection
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific detail that's both funny and revealing about Jack's past

jokes.text: In fact, I've coined a new term to describe what Elisa is to me... The One.
jokes.setup: Jack claims to have coined a new term
jokes.punchline: It's the extremely common phrase 'The One'
jokes.explanation: Jack's obliviousness to how common this phrase is perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: Yeah, you should be a writer.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion he become a writer
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response but fairly standard

jokes.text: Whoa! Check out the bling... or whatever it's called now.
jokes.punchline: Uncertainty about current slang terminology
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about generational language gaps

jokes.text: Physical comedy sequence with ring getting stuck in vent
jokes.setup: Character reaches for something near a vent
jokes.punchline: Gets finger caught in the vent grate
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: She's very spirited... like a show horse.
jokes.punchline: Comparison to a show horse
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate analogy that's both affectionate and slightly condescending

jokes.text: Oh, no, no. She's not the bride. This is the one I am marrying.
jokes.setup: Salesperson assumes the energetic woman is the bride
jokes.punchline: Jack clarifies the quiet one is his fiancée
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that reveals character dynamics

jokes.text: Please follow me to the real showroom.
jokes.punchline: Implies they were in a fake/cheap showroom before
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on retail discrimination based on perceived wealth

jokes.text: Oh, Miss Lemon, we can't have coconut products out anymore because of staff allergies. / What? No, allergies are psychosomatic.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explains coconut ban due to allergies
jokes.punchline: Liz dismisses all allergies as psychological
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon skepticism and dismissiveness of others' problems

jokes.text: The only reason I'm allergic to dogs is because one bit me the first time I got my period.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her theory about psychosomatic allergies
jokes.punchline: Oversharing an embarrassing personal detail
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz overshare that's both gross and weirdly specific to her character

jokes.text: If I have a strawberry, my throat shuts up faster than a girl in math class.
jokes.punchline: Comparison to girls being silenced in math class
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected comparison that works on multiple levels - throat shutting/being shut up

jokes.text: You know, if my cousin Stephanie eats a walnut her throat shuts up faster than a Filipino at a... / Guys! Come on. Not okay.
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off before completing the offensive joke
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Kenneth doesn't understand why his previous joke was problematic

jokes.text: Don't worry, no food in here, Kenneth... just a donut box with a mouse in it.
jokes.setup: Reassuring Kenneth there's no unauthorized food
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually a prank with a mouse
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection setup for prank comedy

jokes.text: She was working at the Dyker Heights Arthur Treacher's and I was residing there.
jokes.setup: Tracy telling how he met his wife
jokes.punchline: Says he was 'residing' at a fast food restaurant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan absurdity - using formal language to describe living in a restaurant

jokes.text: It's either going to be a denim jacket that says 'Hot Bitch' in diamonds... Or a Slanket.
jokes.setup: Tracy needs to pick a special anniversary gift
jokes.punchline: The two wildly different options he's considering
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between extremely expensive tacky gift and cheap practical one

jokes.text: You are wise, Liz Lemon like a genetically-manipulated shark.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to a genetically-manipulated shark
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Tracy Jordan nonsensical but oddly poetic comparison

jokes.text: I'll take that Slanket, too, if you're not going to use it.
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately trying to claim the Slanket too
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon greediness for comfort items

jokes.text: Actually, Lemon... three weeks ago.
jokes.setup: Liz asking when Elisa got back
jokes.punchline: She's been back for three weeks secretly
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation that complicates the situation

jokes.text: I'm afraid he's going to want to go through with his 'proposings' at marriage. Sorry. I haven't spoken English in two menses.
jokes.setup: Elisa explaining her fear about Jack
jokes.punchline: Malapropisms and saying 'two menses' instead of 'two months'
jokes.explanation: Good character humor with language barriers and unintentional medical terminology

jokes.text: Are you a man? / Really? That's your guess? A man?
jokes.punchline: Liz's first guess is that she's a man
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's pop culture-influenced assumptions about 'terrible secrets'

jokes.text: You want to see me naked? / Sort of.
jokes.setup: Elisa offering to prove she's not a man
jokes.punchline: Liz's honest awkward response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon awkward honesty in an uncomfortable situation

jokes.text: I don't think I have the strength to tell it to his head.
jokes.setup: Elisa can't tell Jack her secret
jokes.punchline: Says 'tell it to his head' instead of 'tell him' or 'face'
jokes.explanation: Mild language barrier humor with awkward phrasing

jokes.text: You're a big fan of mine and you're not gay? / Not even bi-curious?
jokes.punchline: Her assumption that all her male fans must be gay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna vanity and assumptions about her fanbase demographics

jokes.text: I still think that would have sold much better if he had shot me in the face.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Phil Spector album sales
jokes.punchline: Jenna thinks getting shot would have helped sales
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Jenna logic about fame through tragedy

jokes.text: But if you had, I would have liked to have gotten that call.
jokes.setup: Fan saying he's glad she wasn't shot
jokes.punchline: But admits he would have wanted to know about it
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the dark humor with honest fan enthusiasm

jokes.text: Jenna, Us Weekly is calling to confirm a story that your animal rescue shelter is supplying quesadilla meat to amusement parks.
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific scandal about pet food becoming human food
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist premise that's specific and disturbing in perfect 30 Rock fashion

jokes.text: All anybody posted on it were penises.
jokes.punchline: User-generated content was exclusively male anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good realistic observation about internet behavior delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Now, don't dare say anything about what I told you about my secret. / I'm not going to because you have to.
jokes.setup: Elisa warning Liz not to tell Jack her secret
jokes.punchline: Liz says she won't because Elisa has to tell him herself
jokes.explanation: Good tension as Liz tries to force Elisa to handle her own problem

jokes.text: I called 911. They wouldn't even connect me to their celebrity service.
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to find the paramedic she met
jokes.punchline: Assumes 911 has a celebrity tier of service
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna entitlement - expecting emergency services to have VIP treatment

jokes.text: She kills her father hoping that the guy will come to that funeral, too. / That's correct... oh, boy.
jokes.setup: Pete tells psychiatric test about sociopaths
jokes.punchline: Jenna gives the sociopathic answer immediately
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation showing Jenna's sociopathic tendencies

jokes.text: But of course, she'd also have to kill her father's doorman and anyone else who might have seen her. And you, for giving her the idea in the first place.
jokes.setup: Jenna already gave the sociopathic answer
jokes.punchline: She continues planning the murder spree in detail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing how thoroughly Jenna thinks through the murder plan

jokes.text: I'm wearing it as a joke.
jokes.punchline: Claims it's intentional comedy
jokes.explanation: Standard character defense for embarrassing fashion choice

jokes.text: I can't have this on my chest scaring off beautiful women in the clubs.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he can't get the tattoo
jokes.punchline: Openly admits he wants to pick up other women
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's shameless infidelity plans

jokes.text: And you know my signature move with the ladies. It's... / Taking off your shirt.
jokes.setup: Tracy about to reveal his signature move
jokes.punchline: Liz flatly states what everyone knows
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Tracy's dramatic buildup with obvious truth

jokes.text: Now, it's a Sophie's Choice.
jokes.punchline: Compares it to the Holocaust novel's impossible choice
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy Jordan misuse of dramatic literary reference for trivial problem

jokes.text: What do you want me to say, Tracy... 'I'm sorry I made it harder for you to cheat on your wife?'
jokes.setup: Tracy wants sympathy for his dilemma
jokes.punchline: Liz sarcastically points out the real issue
jokes.explanation: Good Liz moral clarity cutting through Tracy's self-pity

jokes.text: My real name... is Dick Whitman.
jokes.punchline: References Mad Men character reveal
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that works for viewers who know Mad Men but fairly cheap

jokes.text: La Viuda Negra! / What? What does that mean? / The Black Widow.
jokes.punchline: Reveals it means 'The Black Widow'
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic reveal with Kenneth's unexpected Spanish knowledge

jokes.text: My current theory is she's the mother of those Michael Jackson kids.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre conspiracy theory about celebrity children
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth random conspiracy thinking with topical celebrity reference

jokes.text: Blue writing on green. Why?
jokes.punchline: Complains about the color scheme instead of language barrier
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack focus on aesthetic details while missing the actual problem

jokes.text: I was married once and I killed my husband.
jokes.setup: Building to reveal of terrible secret
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of murder
jokes.explanation: Excellent shocking reveal delivered with perfect casual tone

jokes.text: They threw my case out. I couldn't get an impartial jury after that song about me came out.
jokes.setup: Jack asking why she's not in jail
jokes.punchline: Her case was thrown out due to a song making her famous
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd legal loophole explanation that feels distinctly 30 Rock

jokes.text: You get the tattoo... but when you hit the clubs you draw a mane around the face with a marker and make it look like a lion named... Tangiers.
jokes.setup: Trying to solve Tracy's tattoo problem
jokes.punchline: Elaborate plan to disguise wife's face as a lion
jokes.explanation: Creative absurd solution with good specific details

jokes.text: if you want everyone to think I own a gay lion! Tangiers?
jokes.punchline: Assumes people will think the lion is gay based on the name
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic about stereotypes and exotic names

jokes.text: The Moroccan national soccer team is the Lions. Tangiers is in Morocco. So, yeah, I guess I'm an idiot.
jokes.punchline: Dotcom reveals the actual logical connection, making Tracy the idiot
jokes.explanation: Good reversal showing Dotcom's intelligence and Tracy's assumptions

jokes.text: I think my grandpa may have but he never really liked to talk about what happened at... Kent State.
jokes.setup: Liz about to mention war experience
jokes.punchline: Reveals grandpa was involved in Kent State shooting
jokes.explanation: Good dark historical reference that recontextualizes family history

jokes.text: I'm 50. To put it in perspective, that's like 32 for ladies.
jokes.punchline: Sexist conversion rate for women's aging
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack casual sexism delivered as helpful explanation

jokes.text: If he was here at night, he must work the evening shift... like on a sheik's pleasure yacht. Thank you.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to yacht staff scheduling
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jenna reference point that reveals her worldview and experience

jokes.text: It's not product placement. I just like it.
jokes.punchline: Defensive about appearing to do product placement
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on TV product placement conventions

jokes.text: What if I find myself stranded in a snow cave with a stern but comely lady geologist, both of us knowing that our only chance for survival is the heat from our naked bodies?
jokes.setup: Jack worried about potential infidelity scenarios
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and elaborate hypothetical seduction scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack romantic fantasy disguised as practical concern - perfectly specific and absurd

jokes.text: Face it, you are the closest thing to a man working here right now.
jokes.punchline: Tells Liz she's the most masculine option available
jokes.explanation: Good insult/compliment that speaks to both characters' traits

jokes.text: And you're going to be tempted like Jesus in the wilderness. / Jesus is my stereo guy and The Wilderness is a club I took him to once.
jokes.setup: Tracy making biblical reference about temptation
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's thinking of actual people named Jesus and The Wilderness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy Jordan logic that recontextualizes biblical reference through his literal worldview

jokes.text: Oh, for God's sakes. What is this, Third Watch?
jokes.punchline: Jenna compares it to medical drama TV show
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna tendency to view real life through TV references

jokes.text: We're gonna have to cut off his hands to get the sickness out. He's gonna have to learn how to urinate with his feet.
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical medical procedure and consequence
jokes.explanation: Good absurd fake medical advice with disturbing logical follow-through

jokes.text: I'm just a girl... standing in front of a boy she poisoned so this other boy would go to town on her.
jokes.punchline: Parodies Notting Hill romance with poisoning plot
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody that transforms romantic movie cliché into sociopathic confession

jokes.text: Thank God... 'sociopath' downgraded to 'extreme narcissist.'
jokes.punchline: Treats psychiatric diagnosis like a credit rating improvement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna perspective treating mental health diagnosis as status improvement

jokes.text: In the 20 years that I've known her... I've never cheated on my wife.
jokes.setup: Tracy building to reveal terrible secret
jokes.punchline: The secret is that he's been faithful
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character revelation that subverts expectations about Tracy's lifestyle

jokes.text: All the phone numbers you see me hand out? They're not even mine.
jokes.punchline: He gives out fake numbers to avoid actual contact
jokes.explanation: Good reveal about how Tracy maintains his image while staying faithful

jokes.text: Hello? No, this isn't Tracy Jordan. Really? I've not heard of that term before. It... do you know how to get to Connecticut?
jokes.setup: Someone calling number Tracy gave out
jokes.punchline: Random person confused by sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the innocent consequences of Tracy's fake number scheme

jokes.text: So can you because I'm a ridiculous, unstable human being.
jokes.punchline: Logic that if unstable Tracy can be faithful, anyone can
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-aware Tracy moment that's both inspiring and self-deprecating

jokes.text: Oh, you mean my mouth radio?
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent term for the instrument
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocent terminology that's both charming and absurd

jokes.text: When you call 911, tell them they have to send everyone.
jokes.setup: Kenneth helping Jenna find the paramedic
jokes.punchline: Suggesting they request all emergency responders
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth helpfulness with completely impractical but logical suggestion

jokes.text: Working on my night cheese, Mmm, mmm...
jokes.punchline: Singing about eating cheese, revealing sad personal habits
jokes.explanation: Iconic Liz Lemon moment that perfectly captures her lonely food habits

jokes.text: How are you so quiet when your parades are so loud?
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about parade volume
jokes.explanation: Good Elisa language confusion creating poetic non-sequitur

jokes.text: I knew your relationship was too weird not to be sexual!
jokes.setup: Elisa seeing Jack with Liz late at night
jokes.punchline: Assumes weirdness equals sexual relationship
jokes.explanation: Good jealous logic that misinterprets the Jack-Liz dynamic

jokes.text: Yeah, I mean, come on. Look at me.
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating reference to her own appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz self-deprecation as defense mechanism

jokes.text: Because she's your bro.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he came to Liz
jokes.punchline: Describes Liz using masculine friendship term
jokes.explanation: Good characterization of Jack-Liz relationship as platonic masculine friendship

jokes.text: Lemon, isn't there a Slanket somewhere you should be filling with your farts? Shoo.
jokes.setup: Jack dismissing Liz from private conversation
jokes.punchline: Gross but accurate description of her comfort habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack insult that combines callback with gross but accurate personal details

jokes.text: And eventually... it makes me loco for Choco-Puffs.
jokes.punchline: Mixes Spanish with cereal commercial catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good bilingual wordplay mixing emotional confession with cereal advertising

jokes.text: Is it my English or was that not a great breaking-up speech?
jokes.punchline: Self-aware critique of his own poor communication
jokes.explanation: Good Jack self-awareness about his communication failures

jokes.text: Sure, I was legally dead for five minutes but I did it for true love.
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing his sacrifice for Jenna
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions being clinically dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth devotion with casual treatment of near-death experience

jokes.text: Wow, that is one gay lion.
jokes.punchline: Confirms earlier prediction about the lion appearing gay
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff to earlier lion tattoo discussion

tmdb_overview: Jack goes shopping for an engagement ring, for Elisa, with Liz. Elisa tells Liz a secret, and a prank results in an injury back at TGS.
insight: Absurdist character comedy sustains 73 jokes across 40 minutes at near-elite efficiency.
